other_observers: Glenn Walbek; I found and identified the bird. Glenn took the pictures.
description: Size/shape identical to the many Swainson's Thrushes in the area. Cheek had an silvery-gray fan of feathers. No warm tones in the face/cheek. Tail was very slightly redder than the back of the bird. Chest white with dark brownish-gray spots. No warm tones in chest. Back and wings all the same flat gray-brown tone.Behaviors: Behaving much like the Swainson's Thrushes in the area. Staying low and largely hidden along the inlet grove.Call: N/APlumage: Adult
